text: Heads up — Molex just notified us that the MX150 connector we spec'd for the battery harness has a 14-week lead time, up from 6 weeks. Build B is at risk if we don't act fast. @u_alice @u_fiona do you have a view on whether we can redesign around a drop-in alt?

The TE Connectivity 1-2316181-1 is a close footprint match. I'll pull the datasheet and check current ratings today. We might need a PCB rev if the pin pitch differs, but the current path should be OK.
Also worth checking Amphenol FCI options. We used their connectors on the Gen2 board and had good yield. I can dig up the part numbers if Alice is heads-down.

Build B cutoff for BOM lock is April 18. That gives us 8 days. If Alice's TE option needs a PCB rev it will slip the build. Can we schedule a 30-min design review by EOD Thursday? @u_carlos please confirm Molex lead time in writing.
Confirmed — Molex is quoting 14 weeks in writing. Will forward the email. Checking Digi-Key for spot stock on the TE part as well.

TE part datasheet looks compatible — pin pitch matches, rated for 20A which is sufficient. The only concern is a slight height difference (8.5mm vs 8.0mm). Flagging for mechanical check before we commit.

Seeing a reproducible hang on the BMS bring-up sequence. After PMIC initialization, the I2C bus to the fuel gauge (MAX17261) is locking up — SCL stuck low. Happens 100% with the current firmware on Rev C hardware. Not seeing it on Rev B. @u_greg have you seen this?

Haven't hit this on my bench, but I'm still on the older bootloader. Let me flash Rev C and replicate. What's your I2C clock config — any chance the PMIC is asserting a POWER_GOOD line that's gating the fuel gauge before the bus is idle?

I2C clock is 400kHz. POWER_GOOD assertion timing is worth checking — that's a plausible cause. Schematic shows a 10kΩ pull-up on SCL; wondering if that's insufficient given Rev C capacitive load changes.

Can confirm I'm seeing the same hang in our automated bring-up suite — 3/3 Rev C units fail at the same point in the sequence. Rev B passes clean. This is a blocker for our validation run scheduled tomorrow morning.

Replicated it. Looks like a timing issue — inserting a 5ms delay after PMIC init clears the hang in my local test. Not a clean fix but confirms the theory. @u_bob let's bisect whether it's pull-up value or init sequence ordering.

Agreed — 5ms delay is a workaround, not a fix. Root cause is likely the pull-up. I'll check schematic vs layout for stray capacitance and propose either a lower pull-up resistor or a firmware sequencing fix. Will update by EOD.
Looping in @u_alice — the Rev C pull-up change might have been introduced in the last schematic update. Can you confirm if R42 was changed between Rev B and Rev C?

Thermal cycling results for the Rev C PCBA are in — we're seeing a 12% failure rate on the voltage regulator (U7) after 50 cycles (−40°C to +85°C). Spec calls for <2% at 100 cycles. This is not going to pass qualification. @u_alice @u_evan is this a layout issue or a component selection issue?

U7 thermal pads were revised in Rev C — reduced copper pour around the pad to address a DRC clearance violation. That could be killing the thermal dissipation. I'd look at the layout first before assuming it's a component issue.

Agreed with Alice. Also worth checking whether the underfill spec changed — if U7 has exposed solder balls that are now experiencing more stress due to the reduced pad area, that explains the early failures. We should pull cross-sections on a few failed units.

This is a qual blocker. We need a root cause analysis and a proposed fix before the program review on April 15. @u_diana please capture this in the risk register and assign severity P1. @u_alice @u_evan can you commit to a prelim RCA by April 12?
Risk register updated. Will pull failed units for cross-section analysis today. Prelim RCA by April 12 is achievable if we have layout files by tomorrow AM.

Decision needed: do we target Rev C board for Build B, or revert to Rev B silicon while we investigate the open items? Inputs from HW and firmware teams by EOD Friday please. Trade-offs: Rev C has the antenna gain improvement we need for FCC, but currently has two open P1 issues.

My vote is Rev C with a targeted PCB spin to fix the thermal pad issue and the pull-up. We lose the antenna improvement if we revert and that's a cert risk. But we need the connector BOM decision resolved first — the MX150 issue affects both revisions.
From firmware perspective: the I2C hang is a workaround-able in software if the pull-up fix isn't feasible before build. So firmware won't be the blocker. +1 for Rev C target.
Agreed on Rev C. Reverting to Rev B silicon would set us back 3 weeks minimum and doesn't resolve the thermal issue either. We should treat this as a PCB rev not a silicon rev decision.
Rev C +1. But the mechanical clearance for the connector height delta needs to be resolved before we lock BOM — flagging that as a dependency on the Rev C decision.

Good alignment — tentative decision is Rev C target for Build B, conditional on connector resolution by April 11 and preliminary RCA on the thermal issue by April 12. Will document in program tracker.
Anyone remember what snacks we're bringing to the team lunch on Friday? I've got leftover BGA rework solder paste I can contribute if needed 😄
I'll bring baozi. And please keep the solder paste away from the food table this time 😂
Bringing chips and salsa. Someone else do dessert?
Brownies.
I'll bring sparkling water — tired of everyone hoarding it from the lab fridge.
I'll grab fruit. And Hana — retire the solder paste joke, it's been 3 sprints 😂

Starting to see intermittent ADC read failures on the environmental sensor (SHT40) during the extended soak test. Manifests as sudden jumps in humidity reading — 45% RH to 0% RH within one sample. Not consistent — maybe 1 in 500 readings. @u_bob is this a firmware issue or do we suspect the sensor?

Intermittent and 1 in 500 makes me think bus noise rather than firmware logic. Is the SHT40 sharing an I2C bus with anything else that's been revised? If you can capture the raw I2C trace I can look at timing.
Same I2C bus as the IMU. On Rev C we added the IMU with faster clock requirements. Could be causing interference if the bus isn't properly segmented. Worth checking the bus topology.
Good call — will capture I2C trace and share. This also might be related to the I2C issue Bob found on the BMS. Same Rev C change? Worth looking at together.

Preliminary update on the NOR flash situation: Winbond is experiencing factory allocation issues affecting the W25Q128. Lead time has moved from 8 weeks to 18 weeks across all major distributors. This is a secondary risk behind the MX150 connector issue but could surface as a problem for DVT2 build. @u_fiona tagging for program awareness.
Logged. What's the DVT2 build date? If it's >18 weeks from now we might be ok, otherwise we need to explore Macronix or ISSI alternatives. Can you check pin-compatibility of the MX25L12833F?

Macronix MX25L12833F is pin and protocol compatible — I've used it on a previous project. The only difference is the status register encoding for write protection, which would need a firmware update. Worth flagging to Bob.
